task_completion.description: The agent creates a 2D sketch of the Venturi nozzle cross-section forming a closed loop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: SolveSpace workspace showing a 2D sketch with horizontal and vertical lines, as well as angled lines for the convergent and divergent sections.

task_completion.description: The agent applies the correct dimensional constraints to the 2D sketch.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dimension labels visible on the sketch matching the target values: 50 (top wall/length), 15 (top wall height), 10 (inlet bore height/length), 3 (throat height), 8 (divergent exit height), 20 (throat start x-coordinate), and 5 (throat length).

task_completion.description: The agent revolves the 2D cross-section 360 degrees around the X-axis to create a 3D solid.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewport displays a 3D cylindrical Venturi nozzle solid, and the property panel shows a 'Revolve' group or operation.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the SolveSpace model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the filename 'venturi_nozzle.slvs' in the '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory, or the SolveSpace window title bar displaying 'venturi_nozzle.slvs'.

task_completion.description: The agent exports the 3D solid as a STEP file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export' dialog showing the filename 'venturi_nozzle.step' in the '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory, or a terminal/file manager window confirming the creation of the .step file.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the SolveSpace GUI to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SolveSpace application open with active drawing, dimensioning, or modeling taking place, rather than just terminal commands or a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent created the model from scratch rather than opening a pre-existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Early screenshots show an empty workspace or a new sketch being started in SolveSpace.

integrity.description: The STEP file was exported via SolveSpace, not created via a script or text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace export dialog is visible, or the menu for exporting 3D geometry is open.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected SolveSpace model path is /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/venturi_nozzle.slvs. The expected exported 3D solid path is /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/venturi_nozzle.step. The target dimensions for the 2D sketch are 50.0, 15.0, 10.0, 3.0, 8.0, 20.0, and 5.0 mm.
